data.title: Monster Beverage Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Monster Beverage Corp, a leading player in the energy drink industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and robust business performance, driven by strategic pricing actions, international market expansion, and new product launches.

Financial Highlights

Monster Beverage Corp reported impressive financial results for the three-month period ended June 30, 2025:

  • Net Sales: $2,111.6 million, reflecting an increase from $1,900.6 million in the same period last year, driven by price increases and international market expansion.
  • Gross Profit: $1,176.4 million, up from $1,019.5 million, benefiting from the Pricing Actions implemented in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Operating Income: $631.6 million, compared to $527.2 million in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $488.8 million, an increase from $425.4 million, supported by higher sales and effective cost management.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Basic: $0.50, up from $0.41, reflecting the company's strong financial performance.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.50, compared to $0.41, demonstrating consistent earnings growth.

Business Highlights

In addition to its financial achievements, Monster Beverage Corp also reported notable business developments:

  • Revenue Segments: The Monster Energy® Drinks segment remains the largest contributor, representing 91.7% of net sales for the three-months ended June 30, 2025. The Strategic Brands segment accounted for 6.2% of net sales, while the Alcohol Brands segment contributed 1.8%, and the Other segment made up 0.3%.
  • Geographical Performance: Net sales to customers outside the United States increased by 15.8% to $864.2 million for the three-months ended June 30, 2025, accounting for approximately 41% of total net sales. This growth was driven by strong performance in international markets despite an unfavorable impact from foreign currency exchange rates.
  • New Product Launches: During the three-months ended June 30, 2025, the company introduced several new products, including Monster Energy® Lando Norris Zero Sugar, Monster Energy® Valentino Rossi Zero Sugar, and a range of Blind Lemon® flavored lemonades.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its growth strategy by further developing domestic markets and expanding its international business. This includes leveraging the recent product launches and pricing actions to enhance market presence and profitability.

SEC Filing: Monster Beverage Corp [ MNST ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Fortinet, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Fortinet, Inc., a global leader in broad, integrated, and automated cybersecurity solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant growth in both financial and operational metrics, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic advancements in the cybersecurity industry.

Financial Highlights

Fortinet, Inc. reported strong financial results for the second quarter and first half of 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Total Revenue: $1,630.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, an increase of 14% compared to the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, total revenue was $3,169.7 million, also a 14% increase year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $1,315.1 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting a 13% increase from the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, gross profit was $2,561.7 million, a 16% increase year-over-year.
  • Operating Income: $458.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up 5% from the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, operating income was $911.8 million, a 20% increase year-over-year.
  • Net Income: $440.1 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, an increase from $379.8 million in the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income was $873.5 million, up from $679.1 million in the same period last year.
  • Net Income Per Share (Basic): $0.57 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.50 in the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income per share (basic) was $1.14, compared to $0.89 in the same period last year.
  • Net Income Per Share (Diluted): $0.57 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.49 in the same period last year. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income per share (diluted) was $1.13, compared to $0.88 in the same period last year.

Business Highlights

Fortinet's business performance in the second quarter of 2025 was marked by significant achievements and strategic initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: Fortinet's revenue is diversified across product lines, with significant contributions from secure networking, unified SASE, and AI-driven SecOps. The company experienced growth in secure networking hardware products and term licenses, contributing to the increase in product revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The EMEA region contributed 41% of total revenue, showing an 18% increase compared to the previous year. The Americas region also contributed 41% of total revenue, with an 11% increase, while the APAC region contributed 18%, marking a 10% increase.
  • Sales Units: Fortinet's product revenue growth was driven by increased sales of hardware products and software licensing, particularly in secure networking hardware products.
  • New Product Launches: Fortinet continues to innovate with its FortiAI and FortiEndpoint solutions, enhancing security operations and endpoint protection. These products integrate advanced capabilities like endpoint detection and response, contributing to the company's competitive edge.
  • New Production Launches: Fortinet expanded its data center operations with new real estate acquisitions in Germany, Canada, the United States, and the Netherlands, enhancing its capacity for data center operations, office space, and warehouse operations.
  • Future Outlook: Fortinet expects continued growth in product and service revenue for the remainder of 2025, driven by secure networking, unified SASE, and AI-driven SecOps. The company anticipates further expansion in its data center footprint and PoPs deployment, supporting its growth strategy.

SEC Filing: Fortinet, Inc. [ FTNT ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Community West Bancshares SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Community West Bancshares, a prominent player in the banking sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial improvements and strategic business expansions, particularly following the acquisition of Community West. This article delves into the key financial and operational highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: Community West Bancshares reported a total interest income of $46.025 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, marking an increase from $43.997 million in the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by higher interest and fees on loans.

Net Interest Income Before Provision for Credit Losses: The company achieved a net interest income before provision for credit losses of $33.304 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $29.057 million in the same period in 2024, indicating improved interest income performance.

Net Income: Community West Bancshares reported a net income of $7.832 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $6.290 million in the same period in 2024, highlighting a significant turnaround in profitability.

Basic Earnings Per Share: The basic earnings per share stood at $0.41 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a loss of $0.33 per share in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved earnings performance.

Diluted Earnings Per Share: Similarly, the diluted earnings per share was $0.41 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a loss of $0.33 per share in the same period in 2024, consistent with basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Community West Bancshares operates primarily in the banking sector, with revenue streams derived from interest and fees on loans, interest on deposits in other banks, and interest and dividends on investment securities. Non-interest income is generated from service charges, net realized losses on sales and calls of investment securities, and other income sources such as service fees and bank-owned life insurance.

Geographical Performance: The acquisition of Community West has expanded the company's footprint in Central California, particularly enhancing its presence in the Central Coast region. This acquisition has diversified the company's commercial banking business and improved its loan-to-deposit ratio.

Sales Units: The company reported a total of $2,397,873,000 in gross loans as of June 30, 2025, with significant contributions from commercial real estate - non-owner occupied, manufactured housing, and commercial real estate - owner occupied segments.

Future Outlook: Community West Bancshares is focused on leveraging the synergies from the acquisition of Community West to enhance operational efficiencies and expand its commercial banking offerings. The management is optimistic about the potential for increased revenue from the expanded geographical presence and diversified product offerings.

SEC Filing: Community West Bancshares [ CWBC ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Allbirds, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Allbirds, Inc., a company known for its sustainable footwear and apparel, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025. The company has faced several challenges, including international distributor transitions and retail store closures, which have impacted its financial results.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenue: Allbirds reported net revenue of $39.7 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $71.8 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This represents a decrease from the same periods in 2024, primarily due to international distributor transitions and retail store closures.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $16.2 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $30.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. Gross margins were 40.7% and 42.6% respectively, indicating a decline from the previous year.

Loss from Operations: Allbirds reported a loss from operations of $(16.5) million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $(39.4) million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This shows a reduction in operating losses compared to the same periods in 2024.

Net Loss: The net loss for the three months ended June 30, 2025, was $(15.5) million, and $(37.4) million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This is an improvement compared to the net loss of $(19.1) million and $(46.5) million for the same periods in 2024.

Net Loss Per Share: The net loss per share was $(1.92) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $(4.64) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(2.45) and $(5.96) for the same periods in 2024, indicating a decrease in loss per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Allbirds generates revenue primarily from sales of footwear and apparel products through direct digital channels and physical retail stores. The majority of sales are through direct channels, with a focus on core franchise products to drive consumer engagement.

Geographical Performance: The United States remains the primary market for Allbirds, contributing significantly to net revenue. International markets also play a role, though specific countries did not exceed 10% of net revenue individually. The company has transitioned operations in several international markets to distributor partners.

Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, Allbirds operates 24 stores, focusing on optimizing the store fleet to drive customer acquisition. The store count has decreased due to strategic closures in the United States and transitions to distributor partners in international markets.

New Product Launches: The company continues to innovate with sustainable materials, focusing on core franchise products to increase closet share among existing customers. New product launches are aligned with Allbirds' commitment to sustainability and comfort.

Future Outlook: Allbirds plans to continue scaling its brand awareness and customer acquisition efforts, focusing on cost-effective strategies. The company aims to optimize its omni-channel strategy and store fleet while maintaining a commitment to sustainability and reducing its carbon footprint.

SEC Filing: Allbirds, Inc. [ BIRD ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: AVITA Medical, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
AVITA Medical, Inc., a regenerative medicine company specializing in the development and commercialization of innovative therapies for skin regeneration, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements for the recent quarter, showcasing the company's robust growth and strategic advancements in the industry.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $18.4 million, increased by 21% compared to the same period in the prior year, driven by deeper penetration within customer accounts and new product launches.

Gross Profit: $14.9 million, representing a gross profit margin of 81.2%, down from 86.1% in the prior year due to volume discounts, higher inventory reserves, and product mix.

Operating Loss: $11.1 million, improved by 29% from the prior year, reflecting a decrease in total operating expenses by 9%.

Net Loss: $9.9 million, improved by 36% from the prior year, with a net loss per common share of $0.38, compared to $0.60 in the prior year.

Net Loss Per Common Share: $0.38, improved from $0.60 in the prior year, reflecting better cost management and increased revenues.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported increased commercial revenues driven by deeper penetration within customer accounts and new accounts for the treatment of traumatic and surgical wounds. The RECELL product line contributed significantly to this growth, with sales revenue from RECELL products reaching $34.5 million for the six-month period.

Geographical Performance: The United States remains the primary market, contributing $35.7 million in revenue for the six-month period. Other regions such as Japan, the European Union, and Australia also showed positive performance, with Japan generating $1.1 million in revenue.

New Product Launches: The company launched Cohealyx, a collagen-based dermal matrix, on April 1, 2025. This product is designed to support cellular migration and revascularization, enhancing wound bed closure readiness.

Future Outlook: The company aims to increase market penetration in U.S. burn centers and expand the adoption of the RECELL portfolio for traumatic and surgical wounds. Additionally, the company plans to conduct post-market studies for Cohealyx and PermeaDerm to support commercialization efforts.

SEC Filing: AVITA Medical, Inc. [ RCEL ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: China narrows gap with US as Germany’s biggest trading partner
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. tariffs take their toll on German exports
  • Chinese imports surge with yuan undervaluation, say analysts
  • German exports to China decline, leading to record trade deficit

China came close to overtaking the United States as Germany's largest trading partner in the first half of 2025, preliminary data from the German statistics office showed, as German exports to the U.S. declined amid higher tariffs.

German imports and exports with the U.S. totalled about 125 billion euros ($145 billion) from January to June, while trade with China reached 122.8 billion euros, according to Reuters calculations.

"Although the U.S. was able to defend its position as Germany’s most important trading partner, the lead over German trade with China is razor-thin," said Vincent Stamer, economist at Commerzbank.

The U.S. had overtaken China as Germany’s top trading partner in 2024, ending an eight-year streak for China. The shift came as Germany sought to reduce its reliance on China, with Berlin citing political differences and accusing Beijing of unfair practices.

Trade dynamics shifted again, however, in 2025 with Donald Trump’s return to the White House and renewed tariffs. The EU’s trade deal with the U.S. in July set tariffs at 15% for most products.

“As the year progresses, losses in German exports to the U.S. are likely to continue and even intensify,” said Juergen Matthes, head of international economic policy at the Cologne Institute for Economic Research.

German exports to the U.S. fell 3.9% to 77.6 billion euros in the first half compared to the same period last year.

Commerzbank expects new U.S. tariffs to slow Germany’s exports to the U.S. by 20% to 25% over the next two years.

“As a result, China is likely to regain the top spot among Germany’s trading partners over the course of the year,” Stamer said.

CHINESE IMPORTS SURGE

Imports from China surged 10.7% year-on-year in the first half, reaching 81.4 billion euros.

“Apparently, German companies and consumers find it difficult to replace Chinese goods,” Stamer said.

The rise may indicate China has begun redirecting trade from the U.S. to Europe, flooding the German and European market with cheaper goods, said Carsten Brzeski, global head of macro at ING.

A significant undervaluation of the yuan against the euro is also making Chinese imports cheaper, said Cologne Institute's Matthes.

German exports to China fell 14.2% to 41.4 billion euros, with exporters struggling amid increased competition from Chinese manufacturers.

The sharp decline in exports to China, combined with surging imports, has led to a record trade deficit of 40 billion euros, second only to 2022.

“All these developments are damaging the German economy and further exacerbating the industrial crisis,” Matthes said.

($1 = 0.8600 euros)

data.title: Copper edges higher on US rate cut hopes and Chinese demand
data.description:
Copper prices crept higher for a third consecutive session on Friday, bolstered by hopes of U.S. interest rate cuts after a central bank appointment and upbeat economic data in China.

Benchmark three-month LME copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.3% at $9,714 a metric ton by 1000 GMT, extending a rebound after touching its lowest in three weeks on July 31.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday announced his pick to fill a vacant seat at the Federal Reserve, boosting hopes of interest rate cuts and weakening the dollar.

A softer dollar makes commodities priced in the U.S. currency less expensive for buyers using other currencies.

"The weaker dollar has been a key driver in August," said Dan Smith at Commodity Market Analytics.

"You've got dollar weakness and China looking like it's in good shape. So the fundamental side feels like it's quite positive for the time being."

Data released on Thursday showed China's exports beat forecasts in July as manufacturers made the most of a fragile tariff truce between Beijing and Washington to ship goods.

The most traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange rose 0.1% to 78,490 yuan ($10,929.02) a ton.

Smith said that LME copper was looking potentially bullish in his algorithmic computer models, which seek to replicate fund activity that place buy and sell orders largely on momentum signals.

"I think there's a chance that next week it will flip back into giving a buy signal on copper, with an upside potentially up towards $10,000," he said.

On the supply side, investors were watching developments in top copper producer Chile, where Codelco has sought permission to reopen a part of its flagship mine after a fatal accident last week.

Other metals were mixed. LME aluminium rose 0.2% to $2,614.50 a ton, zinc added 0.4% to $2,823.50 and tin was little changed at $33,740 while nickel eased 0.1% to $15,100 and lead was down 0.5% at $2,000.

For the top stories in metals, click on

($1 = 7.1818 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Gray Media Q2 revenue falls 7%, but beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Gray Media Q2 revenue falls 7% yr/yr, but beats analysts' expectations

  • Net loss of $69 mln due to cyclical decrease in political advertising revenue

  • Adjusted EBITDA declines, impacted by reduced political advertising revenue

Outlook

  • Company expects Q3 core advertising revenue to decline due to effects of Olympics in year-ago period

  • Gray forecasts Q3 political advertising revenue at $6 mln to $7 mln

  • Company anticipates full-year interest expense of $460 mln

  • Gray sees full-year capital expenditures excluding Assembly Atlanta at $85 mln to $90 mln

Result Drivers

  • POLITICAL ADVERTISING - Revenue decreased by 81% due to the off-year in the political advertising cycle

  • NETWORK AFFILIATION LOSS - Non-cash impairment of $28 mln due to non-renewal of CBS network affiliation at WANF station

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex and the broader NSE Nifty 50 index both declined 1% each, as U.S. tariffs and muted earnings dampened investor sentiment.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was marginally higher versus the U.S. dollar at 87.6550. On a weekly basis it ended lower for a fifth straight week, as trade tensions between India and the United States escalated following President Donald Trump's call for new tariffs on Indian goods.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 99.6475 rupees, with the yield marginally lower at 6.3778%, as investors awaited fresh triggers.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was flattish at 5.48%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dropped 3 bps to 5.645%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.23%.

AMERICAS CARMART INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Americas Car-Mart Inc., a leading player in the used automotive retail industry, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing significant improvements in financial performance, strategic initiatives, and operational highlights for the fiscal year ending April 30, 2025. The company, which operates primarily in small cities throughout the South-Central United States, has shown resilience and strategic foresight in navigating a competitive and regulated market environment.

Financial Highlights

Americas Car-Mart Inc. reported total revenues of $1,390.9 million, reflecting a slight decrease of 0.2% compared to the previous fiscal year, primarily due to a decline in retail units sold. Despite this, the company achieved a gross profit of $664.9 million, with a gross margin of 36.7%, an improvement from 34.7% in the prior year. This was driven by vehicle pricing discipline and cost reductions.

The company reported an income before income taxes of $22.8 million, a significant turnaround from a loss of $40.1 million in the previous year. Net income also saw a substantial improvement, reaching $17.9 million compared to a net loss of $31.4 million in the prior year. This improvement is attributed to better credit performance and effective cost management. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) were $2.33, compared to a loss of $4.92 per share in the previous year, reflecting the company's enhanced profitability.

Business Highlights

The company sold 57,022 retail units in fiscal 2025, a decrease of 1.7% from the previous year. However, the average retail sales price increased to $19,398 per unit. As of April 30, 2025, Americas Car-Mart operated 154 dealerships, primarily in small cities with populations of 50,000 or fewer, throughout the South-Central United States.

Approximately 46% of customers make payments in person at a dealership, fostering regular face-to-face interactions that build trust and encourage timely payments. The company has also invested in a new loan origination system and an enterprise resource planning system to enhance operational productivity and flexibility.

Looking ahead, the company plans to continue expanding its footprint through strategic acquisitions and expects continued pressure on the supply and price of used vehicles in the near term. It also anticipates ongoing investment in workforce development to support growth.

Strategic Initiatives

Americas Car-Mart has focused on improving vehicle quality and optimizing its inventory supply chain through a strategic partnership with a leading automotive services and technology provider. This partnership is expected to enhance vehicle reconditioning and auction facilities, thereby improving vehicle offerings and reducing repair costs. Additionally, the company has implemented a new loan origination system to centralize loan information, supporting better credit management and decision-making.

The company completed an underwritten public offering of 1,700,000 shares of common stock, raising approximately $73.8 million, which was used to pay down a portion of the revolving line of credit. Furthermore, the company entered into a $150 million amortizing warehouse agreement backed by finance receivables, which was used to pay down outstanding amounts under the revolving credit facilities. The revolving credit facility was amended to extend the maturity date to March 31, 2027, and increase total permitted borrowings to $350 million.

Looking forward, the company anticipates continued growth in finance receivables, which will modestly exceed revenue growth due to longer term lengths of installment sales contracts. The strategic partnership and new loan origination system are expected to contribute to improved credit performance and operational efficiency.

Challenges and Risks

Americas Car-Mart operates in the highly competitive and fragmented used automotive retail industry, focusing on the 'Integrated Auto Sales and Finance' segment. The company faces several significant risks, including economic and market risks, inventory and supply chain risks, regulatory risks, operational risks, technological and cybersecurity risks, and climate change risks.

Economic disruptions, such as high inflation and interest rate changes, could adversely affect consumer demand and increase costs, impacting profitability. The company's focus on non-prime customers heightens the risk of delinquencies and credit losses during economic downturns. Additionally, a reduction in vehicle availability or increased acquisition costs could negatively impact gross margins.

The company is subject to federal, state, and local regulations, and non-compliance could result in significant penalties. Geographic concentration in certain states exposes the company to local economic conditions, and its growth strategy depends on favorable operating performance, successful acquisitions, and the ability to attract and retain qualified personnel. The company also relies on information systems for operations, and any failure or security breach could disrupt business and harm its reputation. Climate-related events and regulatory changes could impact operations and consumer preferences, affecting vehicle sales and pricing.

Management is focused on enhancing operational efficiency, expanding through strategic acquisitions, and maintaining strong customer relationships to mitigate risks associated with credit losses and market competition. The company is actively monitoring market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and economic volatility, and adjusting strategies to ensure financial stability and growth.

SEC Filing: AMERICAS CARMART INC [ CRMT ] - 10-K - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Rupee logs worst losing streak in 6 months on US tariff woes; RBI caps damage
data.description:
The Indian rupee ended lower for a fifth straight week, its biggest consecutive weekly drop in six months, as trade tensions between India and the United States escalated following President Donald Trump's call for new tariffs on Indian goods.

The rupee closed marginally higher at 87.6550 against the U.S. dollar, from 87.7025 on Thursday.

The currency opened at 87.5600 and reached an intraday high of 87.5350, supported by unwinding of long dollar positions in the NDF market.

Dollar bids, primarily from oil importers, pushed the USD/INR higher after initial lows, traders said.

For the week, the rupee eased 0.1%, following a 1.2% drop last week, and has depreciated nearly 3% over the past five weeks.

Rising pressure on the rupee was driven by India’s position among the hardest-hit countries in Trump's trade offensive, including a new 25% tariff on Indian goods.

The move places India alongside Brazil, facing the steepest import duties, unsettling markets concerned about the impact on capital flows and investor sentiment towards Indian assets.

Fears of a record low in the currency prompted the Reserve Bank of India to intervene almost daily, preventing a deeper slide, traders said.

The RBI resumed intervention in the NDF market to manage rupee volatility, four bankers told Reuters.

Market participants expect another drop in foreign exchange reserves that saw a decline of more than $9 billion in week ended August 1, indicating intervention in the spot market.

Meanwhile, some remain hopeful that a resolution would be achieved in coming period.

Looking at the recent history, there is a high probability that the U.S. lowers tariffs in coming weeks or months and this could lead to a relief rally in Indian markets, as and when it happens, said Nishit Master, portfolio manager at Axis Securities PMS.

data.title: KHD Humboldt Wedag International AG: KHD raises Earnings Forecast
data.description:
KHD Humboldt Wedag International AG / Key word(s): Change in Forecast

KHD Humboldt Wedag International AG: KHD raises Earnings Forecast

08-Aug-2025 / 12:08 CET/CEST

Disclosure of an inside information acc. to Article 17 MAR of the Regulation (EU) No 596/2014, transmitted by EQS News - a service of EQS Group.

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

Cologne, Germany, August 8, 2025 – KHD Humboldt Wedag International AG (KHD), Cologne, Germany, is raising the KHD Group’s earnings forecast for the 2025 financial year in light of the updated corporate planning and the positive development of the earnings position in the 2025 financial year to date. In contrast to the outlook published in March, in which slightly negative values were expected for the operating result (EBIT) and the EBIT margin, KHD now plans to achieve clearly positive values for EBIT and the EBIT margin in the 2025 consolidated financial statements. Taking into account a positive financial result, KHD is thus planning significantly positive earnings before taxes (EBT) and Group net income for the year. However, due in particular to the unfavorable development of the exchange rate of the Indian rupee against the euro, revenue will be lower than forecast. KHD expects revenue to be up to 20% below the figure for the 2024 financial year. End of Inside Information

08-Aug-2025 CET/CEST The EQS Distribution Services include Regulatory Announcements, Financial/Corporate News and Press Releases.

data.title: Middle East Crude-Benchmarks fall on rising supply amid demand concerns
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmarks Oman, Dubai and Murban fell on Friday and posted weekly losses, weighed by rising OPEC+ supply and concerns over demand.

Oil prices were stable on Friday but poised for the steepest weekly losses since late June on a tariff-hit economic outlook and a potential meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin.

In its latest tender, IOC bought 2 million barrels of U.S. Mars crude, 2 million barrels of Brazilian grades and another 1 million barrels of Libyan crude on a delivered basis, the sources said.

BP sold the high-sulphur Mars crude cargo at $1.5-$2 a barrel above September Dubai quotes, they added.

European trader Petraco sold the 1 million barrels of Libyan Sarir and Mesla crude and Totsa, the trading arm of TotalEnergies , sold the 2 million barrels of Brazilian Sepia and Sururu crude, the sources said. The prices for these cargoes were not immediately available.

Those deals follow IOC's purchase of 8 million barrels of September delivery crude from the Middle East, United States, Canada and Nigeria via tenders in the past week.

India's second biggest state refiner BPCL BPCL.NS> bought 9 million barrels of oil through negotiations for September arrival, a source familiar with the purchases said.

That included 1 million barrels of Angola Girassol, 1 million barrels of U.S. Mars, 3 million barrels of Abu Dhabi Murban and 2 million barrels of Nigerian oil, he said.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps fell 34 cents to $2.54 a barrel.

Unipec will deliver an October-loading Upper Zakum crude cargo to Trafigura following the deals.

At least three vessels that oil major Chevron had used to transport Venezuelan crude to the U.S. were navigating toward the South American country's waters on Thursday, with exports expected to resume later this month following a new U.S. license, according to shipping data and sources.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving Most Today: Trade Desk, Expedia, SoundHound, Pinterest, Twilio, — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

Stock futures were pointing higher while the dollar fell early Friday, ending a week marked by trade developments and geopolitical events, as well as a flurry of company earnings.

These stocks were making moves in premarket trading Friday:

Trade Desk stock plummeted 29% despite posting solid second-quarter earnings. The ad-tech company announced that CFO Laura Schenkein will be replaced by Alex Kayyal — a board member who spent nearly a decade at Salesforce in executive roles — on Aug. 21. Revenue came in at $694 million, up 19% from last year and ahead of expectations. Adjusted earnings per share were in line with expectations at 41 cents. The company also forecast revenue of at least $717 million in the current quarter, also in line with estimates.

Expedia Group soared 18% after the travel booking company raised its full-year outlook on the back of second-quarter earnings that beat expectations late Thursday. Adjusted earnings per share came in at $4.24 on revenue of $3.8 billion, beating analysts's call of $3.97 a share on revenue of $3.7 billion and driven by strong business travel demand. It now sees 2025 revenue and gross booking growth of between 3% and 5% each, up from a range between 2% and 4%, previously.

Pinterest shares fell 13% after the photo-sharing website disappointed Wall Street with its latest earnings report. Second-quarter earnings were 33 cents a share on revenue of $998 million, below the of 35 cents a share on revenue of $975 million expected by analysts, according to FactSet.

Twilio reported earnings that beat expectations late Thursday, sending shares of the cloud communications company up 13% early Friday. Revenue in the second quarter rose 13% year-over year to $1.2 billion while adjusted earnings per share came in at $1.19, comfortably ahead of estimates of $1.05 a share, according to FactSet.

Gilead Sciences stock rose 2.9% after it raised guidance on the back of strong demand for its HIV medicine and solid quarterly earnings late Thursday. CEO Daniel O'Day told Barron's that the recent launch of its HIV prevention shot Yeztugo had started off well.

Under Armour and Wendy's were also slated to report quarterly earnings ahead of the bell Friday.

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Portugal Trade Deficit Rises in June
data.description:
Portugal’s trade deficit widened to €2.35 billion in June 2025 from €2.01 billion a year earlier, as imports rose 3.9% to €8.90 billion while exports slipped 0.1% to €6.55 billion.

Import growth was driven by food products (+12.9%), mainly agricultural goods from Spain; transport equipment (+9.8%), largely passenger cars; and consumer goods (+8.7%).

Industrial supplies fell 3.7%, mostly chemical products from Ireland tied to processing transactions without ownership transfer.

Imports from Spain rose 5.5%, while those from Ireland plunged 73.1%.

On the export side, fuels and lubricants dropped 28.3%, notably to Spain.

Industrial supplies fell 10.8% (-4.8% excluding processing-related trade), mainly chemical products to the US. Offsetting some declines, transport equipment exports surged 19.9%, especially to Germany.

Year-on-year, exports to the US sank 39.4%, while sales to Germany jumped 16.4%.

data.title: Lamar Advertising: 2nd-Half Revenue Improvement Not to Degree Expected Entering Year >LAMR
Hyatt Hotels Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Hyatt Hotels Corp, a leading global hospitality company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market expansion efforts.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $1,808 million, reflecting an increase from $1,703 million in the prior year period, driven by higher base management fees and incentive management fees.

Net Income (Loss): $(4) million, compared to $359 million in the prior year period, primarily due to increased transaction and integration costs.

Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Hyatt Hotels Corporation—Basic: $(0.03) per share, down from $3.55 per share in the prior year period.

Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Hyatt Hotels Corporation—Diluted: $(0.03) per share, down from $3.46 per share in the prior year period.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

The company reported revenues from various segments including base management fees, incentive management fees, franchise and other fees, owned and leased properties, distribution, and other revenues. Notably, base management fees increased from $100 million to $113 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024.

Geographical Performance

As of June 30, 2025, Hyatt's hotel portfolio included 1,487 hotels across 80 countries, with 729 hotels located in the United States. The company continues to expand its presence in international markets, particularly in all-inclusive resorts.

Sales Units

The hotel portfolio comprised 363,790 rooms globally, with 167,446 rooms in the United States and 59,644 rooms in all-inclusive resorts, indicating a strong focus on expanding room capacity.

New Product Launches

The acquisition of Playa Hotels & Resorts N.V. added 15 all-inclusive resorts to Hyatt's portfolio, enhancing its offerings in Mexico, the Dominican Republic, and Jamaica.

Future Outlook

The company plans to sell the Playa Hotels Portfolio for $2,000 million, which includes a $200 million preferred equity investment and contingent consideration. This sale is expected to close by the end of 2025, subject to regulatory approval in Mexico.

Operational Expansion

The acquisition of Bahia Principe and Standard International has expanded Hyatt's management and franchising capabilities, adding new brands and properties to its portfolio.

Management Insights

Management highlighted the strategic importance of expanding the all-inclusive segment and enhancing the lifestyle portfolio through acquisitions and new brand introductions.

SEC Filing: Hyatt Hotels Corp [ H ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: FOX FACTORY HOLDING CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
FOX FACTORY HOLDING CORP, a leading designer and manufacturer of high-performance ride dynamics products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial and operational performance, showcasing growth across various segments and geographical markets, as well as strategic initiatives aimed at future expansion.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: FOX FACTORY HOLDING CORP reported net sales of $374.9 million for the three months ended July 4, 2025, an increase from $348.5 million for the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by robust performance across all business segments.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $117.0 million for the three months ended July 4, 2025, up from $111.0 million in the prior year, reflecting improved cost management and operational efficiency.

Income (loss) from operations: Income from operations was $18.5 million for the three months ended July 4, 2025, slightly down from $18.6 million in the previous year, impacted by increased operating expenses.

Net Income (loss) attributable to FOX stockholders: Net income attributable to FOX stockholders was $2.7 million for the three months ended July 4, 2025, a decrease from $5.4 million in the prior year, primarily due to higher interest expenses and other costs.

Earnings (net loss) per share - Basic: Basic earnings per share were $0.07 for the three months ended July 4, 2025, compared to $0.13 for the same period in 2024, reflecting the impact of increased expenses on net income.

Earnings (net loss) per share - Diluted: Diluted earnings per share were consistent with the basic EPS at $0.07 for the three months ended July 4, 2025, indicating no significant dilution effects.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Powered Vehicles Group generated $123.5 million in net sales for the three months ended July 4, 2025, with products sold under the FOX and Marzocchi brands. The Aftermarket Applications Group reported $114.1 million in net sales, offering products under brands like BDS Suspension and Zone Offroad. The Specialty Sports Group achieved $137.2 million in net sales, with offerings under the FOX, Race Face, and Marzocchi brands.

Geographical Performance: North America remains the largest market with $271.7 million in net sales for the three months ended July 4, 2025. Europe showed significant growth with $58.1 million in net sales, reflecting increased demand. Asia also experienced growth, contributing $38.7 million in net sales.

Sales Units: The company reported a total inventory of $412.8 million as of July 4, 2025, indicating a strong production volume to meet market demand.

New Product Launches: The acquisition of Marzocchi Suspension S.r.l. has expanded the company's product offerings in motorcycle suspension, complementing its existing powered vehicle businesses.

Future Outlook: FOX FACTORY HOLDING CORP plans to continue leveraging its technologies and brand to expand into new categories and end-markets, with a focus on increasing aftermarket penetration and accelerating international growth.

SEC Filing: FOX FACTORY HOLDING CORP [ FOXF ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025
